outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Trump unlikely to accept Iran's latest proposal to end war, sources say
outlets.summary: CNN leads with Trump reviewing but likely rejecting Iran's Hormuz reopening proposal. Satellite imagery shows continued Israeli demolitions in Lebanon during truce. Rubio rejects deal without nuclear preconditions. Brent surges past $110.

outlets.name: NBC News
outlets.headline: Trump is facing an increasingly patient Iran as oil prices spike and peace talks stall
outlets.summary: NBC frames Iran as outlasting US pressure — analytical lens suggesting Tehran's patience strategy may be working. Oil spike to $107.97 underscores economic costs of stalemate. Iran's new proposal positions Tehran as reasonable party.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump bashes Iran peace proposal as Tehran looks to Russia for backing
outlets.summary: Fox News leads with Trump's Truth Social post declaring Iran in a 'state of collapse' and attacking German Chancellor Merz over nuclear posture. Rubio's 'economic nuclear weapon' framing of Iran's Hormuz tactic central. IDF strikes ~20 Beqaa/Qantara targets featured.

outlets.name: Daily Wire
outlets.headline: Israel secretly deployed Iron Dome to UAE — first foreign deployment
outlets.summary: Right-leaning outlets emphasize Israel-UAE military cooperation as vindication of Abraham Accords legacy. Iron Dome success story. Bennett-Lapid alliance seen as political threat to Netanyahu.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war live: Trump reviews peace plan; UN calls for Hormuz to reopen
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera liveblog leads with Trump reviewing but likely rejecting Iran's Hormuz proposal. Guterres' appeal to 'let ships pass' featured prominently. Merz's 'humiliated' comment framed as deepening transatlantic rift. Hezbollah drone attacks on IDF featured as ceasefire collapse accelerating.

outlets.name: BBC
outlets.headline: King Charles arrives in Washington for state visit as Iran war dominates agenda
outlets.summary: BBC frames King Charles visit through prism of UK-US relations strained by Iran war. 29 MPs called for cancellation. Trump-Starmer Hormuz call signals UK-France initiative on freedom of navigation.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: IDF strikes Lebanon's Beqaa Valley for first time in 3 weeks amid shaky ceasefire; destroys two vast Hezbollah tunnels in Qantara
outlets.summary: Times of Israel leads with the largest IDF strike wave since the April 16 ceasefire extension — ~20 Hezbollah targets across Beqaa Valley and southern Lebanon plus destruction of two ~2km cross-border tunnels in Qantara. IDF says tunnels were built with 'direct guidance from Iran.' Netanyahu's 'uranium will be removed by agreement or in resumed fighting' featured.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Report: Israel sent Iron Dome battery, troops to help defend UAE during Iran war
outlets.summary: Haaretz emphasizes the unprecedented Israel-Gulf military cooperation. Analysis frames this as new era of Middle East security architecture. Concern about provoking further Iranian escalation against Gulf states.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Sheikh Qassem: Lebanon truce impossible without Iran's push in Islamabad talks
outlets.summary: Press TV leads with Hezbollah's Qassem crediting Iran for the ceasefire and rejecting direct talks with Israel. Frames Pezeshkian's 'will not negotiate under pressure' statement as principled stance. Araghchi's Putin meeting portrayed as Iran securing key ally against US pressure.

outlets.name: Tehran Times
outlets.headline: Iran proposes Hormuz reopening — nuclear program talks to follow in separate track
outlets.summary: Iranian media frames the new proposal as Iran taking the initiative for peace while US remains bellicose. Blockade framed as the primary obstacle. Commercial flight resumption highlighted as victory narrative.
